title: lemna vulgaris
intro: Lemna vulgaris, also known as the lesser duckweed or common duckweed, is a floating aquatic plant that belongs to the Lemnaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting liver health. Lemna vulgaris can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
